title: Unplug the Dryer
description: Disconnect the dryer from its power source. Pull the plug from the wall outlet or switch off the breaker at the panel.

title: Turn Off Gas (If Applicable)
description: For gas dryers, locate the gas shut-off valve behind the unit and turn it to the closed position before any work.

title: Gather Tools
description: You'll need a 1/4" nut driver, Phillips screwdriver, multimeter, work gloves, and a putty knife or flathead screwdriver.

title: Pull Dryer Away from Wall
description: Carefully slide the dryer forward to access the back panel. Disconnect the vent hose from the exhaust port.

title: Remove Back Panel Screws
description: Using the 1/4" nut driver, remove all screws securing the rear access panel. Set screws aside in a magnetic tray.

title: Remove Back Panel
description: Lift and pull the back panel away from the dryer body. Set it down gently — it may have sharp edges.

title: Locate the Heating Element
description: With the back panel removed, locate the heating element canister — a large metal housing usually mounted on the lower-right interior wall.

title: Disconnect Wiring Harness
description: Carefully disconnect the wire connectors from the heating element terminals. Take a photo first so you remember the wiring layout.

title: Remove Element Mounting Screws
description: Remove the screws or clips holding the heating element canister to the dryer frame. Support the canister so it doesn't drop.

title: Extract Old Heating Element
description: Slide the heating element canister out of the dryer. Inspect the coil — look for breaks, burns, or sagging sections.

title: Install New Heating Element
description: Slide the new element into the canister housing. Ensure the coil sits properly in the ceramic insulators without touching the metal walls.

title: Secure Canister to Frame
description: Reattach the heating element canister to the dryer frame with the original mounting screws. Tighten firmly but don't strip.

title: Reconnect Wiring Harness
description: Reattach all wire connectors to the heating element terminals, matching the photo you took earlier. Push each connector until it clicks.

title: Reattach Back Panel
description: Align the back panel to the dryer body and reinstall all screws. Ensure no wires are pinched between the panel and frame.

title: Reconnect Vent Hose
description: Reattach the dryer vent hose to the exhaust port and secure with the hose clamp. Push the dryer back toward the wall.

title: Continuity Test — New Element
description: Set your multimeter to continuity mode. Touch probes to the heating element terminals. You should get a reading (not OL/open).

title: Thermal Fuse Check
description: Test the thermal fuse for continuity. If it reads open (OL), replace it — a blown thermal fuse will prevent the dryer from heating.

title: High-Limit Thermostat Check
description: Test the high-limit thermostat for continuity. Replace if open. This safety device cuts power to the element if it overheats.

title: Power On & Run Test Cycle
description: Plug the dryer back in (or flip the breaker). Run a timed dry cycle on high heat for 5–10 minutes. Verify hot air exits the vent.

title: Confirm & Clean Up
description: If heat is working, congratulations! Clean lint from the interior, replace the lint trap, and slide the dryer back into final position.
